panels.title: INT-XD — mean hop latency per switch (relative)
panels.description: INT-XD per-switch in-switch latency = egress_tstamp - ingress_tstamp, averaged over the range. Every on-path switch exports independently. Host-clock derived (BMv2) -> relative (not absolute network delay). The raw INT hop_delay field unusable on BMv2 (constant ~1.92 s), so latency is computed from the captured timestamps.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> mean()
  |> keep(columns: ["switch_id", "_value"])

panels.title: INT-XD — max queue occupancy per switch
panels.description: INT-XD per-switch q_occupancy (max over range). Idle emulation → expected 0; rises only under congestion.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "q_occupancy")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> max()
  |> keep(columns: ["switch_id", "_value"])

panels.title: Reports received by mode (current range)
panels.description: Count of reports (seq field) per int_mode — MD and XD volume side by side. XD = one report per on-path switch per packet; MD = one end-to-end report per packet at the sink.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group(columns: ["int_mode"])
  |> count()
  |> keep(columns: ["int_mode", "_value"])

panels.title: INT-XD — hop latency per switch (relative, live)
panels.description: Same per-switch computed latency as the bar gauge, over time. Needs continuous traffic to show width; short burst appears as a single spike.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: INT-MD — end-to-end path delay (live)
panels.description: INT-MD end-to-end delay computed at the sink across the whole path (the 'delay' field). MD's distinctive output — XD has no end-to-end (reports per switch). Host-clock derived -> relative.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "MD" and r._field == "delay")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])

options.content: **INT-XD scaling story.** Report rate tracks offered load (e.g. 41 → 416 pps). Hop latency stays flat and queue occupancy ~0 → the switch is far from saturation; report loss seen here is collector-side, not switch congestion. `seq` is a single global per-report counter on the switch, so gaps in arrival order = dropped reports between switch and collector.

panels.title: INT-XD — report rate per switch (reports/s)
panels.description: Reports/s per switch_id = count of reports per window / window seconds. Should scale ~linearly with offered packet rate; proves the pipeline keeps up.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: count, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: float(v: r._value) / (float(v: int(v: v.windowPeriod)) / 1000000000.0) }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: INT-XD — dropped reports (seq gaps, global)
panels.description: Estimated lost reports per window from gaps in the global seq counter (consecutive arrival delta - 1, summed). 0 = no loss. Rises if the collector can't keep up with the report rate.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group()
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> difference(nonNegative: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: if r._value > 1.0 then r._value - 1.0 else 0.0 }))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])

panels.title: INT-XD — hop latency percentiles (all switches)
panels.description: p50 / p95 / p99 of per-switch hop latency (egress_tstamp - ingress_tstamp) across all switches. Flat tails under rising load = no congestion; a growing p99 would signal queueing.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: (tables=<-, column) => tables |> quantile(q: 0.50), createEmpty: false)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "p50")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: (tables=<-, column) => tables |> quantile(q: 0.95), createEmpty: false)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "p95")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: (tables=<-, column) => tables |> quantile(q: 0.99), createEmpty: false)
  |> set(key: "_field", value: "p99")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "_field"])

panels.title: INT-XD — latency stats per switch (ns)
panels.description: Per-switch mean hop latency + report count over the range. Computed cheaply as mean(egress_tstamp) - mean(ingress_tstamp) (exact by linearity, no per-point pivot), so it renders under high volume. Mean shows the fixed per-port offset (switch 1 higher); see the percentiles panel for tail/max.

targets.query:
mean_lat = from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id", "_field"])
  |> mean()
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ switch_id: r.switch_id, _field: "mean_ns", _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group()

reports = from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "egress_tstamp")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> count()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ switch_id: r.switch_id, _field: "reports", _value: float(v: r._value) }))
  |> group()

union(tables: [mean_lat, reports])
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["switch_id"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> group()

panels.title: INT-XD — queue occupancy per switch (live)
panels.description: Per-switch q_occupancy over time. Stays ~0 at these rates (no congestion); plot it next to latency to show that flat latency == empty queues.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "q_occupancy")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: INT-XD — reports per flow
panels.description: Report count grouped by inner flow (srcip -> dstip). Shows which monitored flows are generating telemetry and their relative volume.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group(columns: ["srcip", "dstip"])
  |> count()
  |> keep(columns: ["srcip", "dstip", "_value"])
  |> group()

panels.title: INT-XD — total report rate (from seq, accurate)
panels.description: True total reports/s = derivative of the global monotonic seq counter. Immune to count-window quantization and to InfluxDB point overwrites, so it's smooth where the per-switch count panel is jagged. seq is chip-wide, so this is the TOTAL across all switches.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "seq")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)
  |> derivative(unit: 1s, nonNegative: true)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])

panels.title: INT-XD — inter-arrival per switch (Δ ingress_tstamp)
panels.description: Time between consecutive packets as seen by the switch hardware = difference of ingress_tstamp per switch. This is the real offered-load pacing (416 pps ~ 2.4 ms, 4k pps ~ 250 us). Legend stdDev = arrival jitter. On Tofino this is ns-precise; on BMv2 it carries host-OS scheduling noise. Note: drops inflate gaps, so read it at rates with ~0 loss.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and r._field == "ingress_tstamp")
  |> group(columns: ["switch_id"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> difference(nonNegative: true)
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "switch_id"])

panels.title: Hardware vs software — mean hop latency by platform
panels.description: Mean in-switch latency (egress_tstamp - ingress_tstamp) grouped by the 'platform' tag. Run the same flow on Tofino (platform=tofino, ns-precise, ~300 ns, flat) and BMv2/Mininet (platform=bmv2, host-clock, larger + noisy). The core HW-vs-SW timestamp story in one panel.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "int_telemetry")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "int_telemetry" and r.int_mode == "XD" and (r._field == "ingress_tstamp" or r._field == "egress_tstamp"))
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r.platform)
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "switch_id", "platform"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r.egress_tstamp - r.ingress_tstamp }))
  |> group(columns: ["platform"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "platform"])
